commit.message:
Core/archetype separation — the big refactor

Architecture: three-layer model
  Core plugin (squad-federated): pure plumbing — worktrees, signals,
  knowledge flow, monitoring, OTel, launch. Knows nothing about what
  teams do.

  Archetype plugins: goal-specific behavior — prompt templates, playbooks,
  completion criteria, cleanup hooks. Installable separately. Per-team.

  User project: domain-specific skills, schemas, data.

Core changes (squad-federated):
- launch.ts: stripped ALL prompt builders. 4-tier prompt resolution:
  --prompt flag → --prompt-file → .squad/launch-prompt.md → generic fallback
- clearArtifacts → resetTeam(): only clears core state (signals).
  Runs .squad/cleanup-hook.sh if archetype provides one.
- Config stripped to 3 fields: branchPrefix, mcpStack, telemetry
- aggregate.ts removed from core (moved to deliverable archetype)

New: squad-archetype-deliverable plugin
- Scatter-gather pattern for producing file artifacts
- Playbook skill: discovery → analysis → deep-dives → validation → distillation
- 3 prompt templates (first/refresh/reset) with {team} placeholders
- cleanup-hook.sh: deletes deliverable + raw/
- aggregate.ts: collects deliverables from all team worktrees

New: squad-archetype-coding plugin
- Implementation teams that produce pull requests
- Playbook skill: design → implement → test → PR
- 3 prompt templates
- cleanup-hook.sh: no-op (code lives in git)

Marketplace: updated with both archetype plugins

commit.message:
fix: use _ensure_keys for generate_password; raise KeePassXCError for get_database_entries (#4)

generate-password does not require test-associate — only change-public-keys
needs to have been done so KeePassXC can encrypt the response. Calling
_ensure_session() caused failures when no association was stored even though
no association is actually needed for password generation.

get_database_entries now raises KeePassXCError with a helpful message instead
of silently returning an empty list when KeePassXC denies the request. The
action requires 'Allow access to all entries' to be enabled in KeePassXC
Settings → Browser Integration.

commit.message:
fix: deploy_to_blog copies featured image PNG+WebP to blog assets (#265)

Root cause of htmlproofer failure: _includes/responsive-image.html in the
blog emits <picture><source srcset="...webp"> for every .png featured image.
deploy_to_blog.py copied charts but never copied featured images at all.

Fix:
1. Create assets/images/ dir in cloned blog repo
2. Copy output/posts/images/{slug}.png → assets/images/{slug}.png
3. Generate assets/images/{slug}.webp via Pillow (quality 85)

This ensures every deployed article has both image variants committed,
preventing htmlproofer failures across all pages that show the article card.

commit.message:
fix: bootc status fails under systemd sandbox restrictions

Three related fixes:

1. iot-updater.service: Add /tmp to ReadWritePaths
   bootc status writes a temp file to /tmp for SELinux install_t type
   testing. Without write access, it silently fails with empty stdout.

2. iot-updater.service: Remove CapabilityBoundingSet
   bootc status needs to enter the host mount namespace via
   /proc/1/ns/mnt. Any explicit CapabilityBoundingSet blocks the
   required capabilities for this, even with CAP_SYS_ADMIN included.
   ProtectSystem=strict + ProtectHome=true provide the actual
   confinement for the sidecar's own writes.

3. sidecar/server.py: Fix stuck error cache in _run_bootc_status()
   The prior code skipped updating _bootc_cache[ts] on repeated
   failures (when data was already set). This caused the cache age to
   grow indefinitely while still retrying bootc on every request.
   Now we only preserve valid (non-error) cached data on failure,
   allowing the error cache to refresh and recover when bootc starts
   working.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: safe softmax in scaled_dot_product_attention to prevent NaN propagation

PyTorch's F.scaled_dot_product_attention returns zeros (not NaN) for
fully-masked rows, while standard IEEE 754 softmax returns NaN when all
logits are -inf (0/0 form).

Add nan_mask fill after softmax to match Python SDPA behavior. This fixes
the no-reference-audio pipeline which was producing 30s of white noise
instead of ~3s of speech.

E2E results (40 steps, seed=42):
  Python PyTorch : 3.160s, RMS=0.111
  Rust LibTorch  : 3.080s, RMS=0.115  ← near-identical
  Rust NdArray   : 6.360s, RMS=0.109  ← expected FP divergence

Also fix: justfile pipeline-real-tch/bf16 recipes missing PATH for venv
python binary (torch-sys needs VIRTUAL_ENV + python in PATH).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: full macOS support with three container runtimes (#8)

* feat: full macOS support with three container runtimes

Add comprehensive macOS support (Apple Silicon and Intel) to yolo-jail,
including Apple's native container CLI as a first-class runtime alongside
Docker and Podman.

## Platform Support

- cli.py: IS_LINUX/IS_MACOS constants gate 20+ platform-specific branches
- entrypoint.py: dual-mode port forwarding (Unix sockets or TCP gateway)
- flake.nix: cross-build Linux images from macOS via imagePkgs/imageSystem
- All 489 unit tests pass, 1 skipped (cgroup lifecycle needs real cgroup v2)
- 27 integration tests pass on macOS with Docker/Colima
- End-to-end verified on Apple Container v0.11.0

## Three Container Runtimes on macOS

Auto-detection priority: container → podman → docker (native-first)

**Docker/Podman** (via Colima or Podman Machine):
- Shared Linux VM, virtiofs file sharing
- TCP gateway for port forwarding (virtiofs rejects Unix sockets)
- Docker named volume for mise (host binaries are Mach-O, not ELF)
- Skip -u UID:GID (VM handles ownership via virtiofs)
- Cgroups read-only inside container

**Apple Container** (native Virtualization.framework):
- Per-container Linux VM with own kernel
- Native --publish-socket for Unix socket forwarding
- Native --cpus/--memory for per-container resource limits
- Auto-defaults to half host CPUs and RAM (min 2 CPU, 4GB)
- Writable cgroups v2 — yolo-cglimit works for per-job limits
- Podman-in-Podman works (/dev/fuse + fuse-overlayfs)
- OCI image conversion via skopeo (no Docker dependency)
- Consolidated ws_state mount to stay under ~22 bind mount limit
- Plain --tmpfs paths (no options syntax)

## macOS Doctor Checks

- Nix daemon connectivity with timeout detection
- Linux builder configuration (remote builder for cross-building)
- Container VM backend status (Colima/Podman Machine)
- Nix store APFS volume mount
- Apple Container system status
- skopeo availability for OCI conversion
- Cgroup limitation notice

## macOS-Specific Fixes

- virtiofs rejects Unix sockets → TCP gateway via host.docker.internal
- /tmp → /private/tmp symlink → Path.resolve() for bind mounts
- Colima tmpfs defaults to mode 755 → explicit --tmpfs /tmp:exec,mode=1777
- Bootstrap stdout leak → redirect to stderr
- Container always gets YOLO_RUNTIME=podman (podman built into image)
- Apple Container ~22 bind mount limit → consolidated /home/agent mount
- Apple Container OCI-only images → skopeo/podman/docker conversion chain

## CI

- check-macos: unit tests on macos-latest (M1 ARM64)
- integration-macos: Docker integration on macos-15-intel (nested virt)
- test_macos_paths.py: 28 tests exercising all IS_MACOS and Apple Container
  code paths with mocked Docker/Nix (run on any platform)

## Documentation

- docs/macos.md: full setup guide (Podman, Docker/Colima, Apple Container)
- docs/platform-comparison.md: 4-column feature matrix, architecture diagrams,
  runtime comparison table, cgroup/nesting analysis
- docs/USER_GUIDE.md: macOS Platform Notes, Apple Container quick start
- README.md: cross-platform feature, runtime config example

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stale container handling and add startup debug banner

- Add find_existing_container() to detect stopped containers (not just running)
- Add _remove_stale_container() to clean up containers from unclean shutdowns
- Remove stale containers under workspace lock before creating new ones
- This also fixes --new when a same-name stopped container exists
- Add _print_startup_banner() showing version, host platform, runtime, and
  container name on stderr for debugging/log sharing
- Print banner in both attach-to-existing and create-new-container paths
- Extract _get_yolo_version() helper to deduplicate version resolution
- Add 15 new tests covering all new functions

* Fix CI: venv symlink test and macOS Nix cross-build

- test_venv_symlinks_resolve: create symlinks targeting in-container /mise
  path instead of host mise path, since host paths don't exist in the jail
- integration-macos: build jail image inside a Linux Docker container
  (via Colima) to avoid needing a Nix Linux builder on the macOS host
- Remove unused host Nix install step from macOS integration job
- Pin nixos/nix Docker image to 2.34.4 for reproducibility
- Bump Colima disk from 20GB to 30GB for Nix store headroom

* Fix Apple Container stale detection and add integration tests

- find_existing_container: use 'container ls --all' for Apple Container
  (default 'ls' only shows running containers, same as Docker 'ps')
- _remove_stale_container: use --force flag for Apple Container
- Add integration tests for stale container recovery (all backends):
  - test_stale_container_auto_removed: creates a stopped container, then
    verifies yolo auto-removes it and starts successfully
  - test_stale_container_new_flag: same with --new flag
  - test_startup_banner_present: verifies banner shows version, platform,
    runtime, and container name

* fix: Apple Container CLI check uses non-existent 'system info' subcommand

The check was calling 'container system info' which doesn't exist in the
Apple Container CLI. Changed to 'container system status' which is the
correct subcommand, and simplified the logic to a single call that checks
both availability and running state.

* fix: remove cgroup warning on macOS — not actionable by user

cgroups are a Linux kernel feature; warning about their absence on macOS
just adds noise to an otherwise clean check output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prefer Apple Container on macOS and fix stale removal message

- Change runtime auto-detection order on macOS to prefer native Apple
  Container over podman/docker (container → podman → docker)
- Fix stale container removal message to print to stderr (where the
  test and startup banner expect it) instead of rich console (stdout)
- Fix tests to mock _is_apple_container() which runs the actual binary

* fix: Apple Container detection fails on 'container CLI version' output

_is_apple_container() checked for 'Apple' in the version string, but
the actual output is 'container CLI version 0.11.0' with no 'Apple'.
Added 'container CLI version' as an alternate match. This caused the
runtime to skip container and fall through to podman (which was broken).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: runtime auto-detection verifies daemon connectivity

Previously, _runtime() and _runtime_for_check() only checked if the CLI
binary existed on PATH. This meant a broken podman (installed but machine
not running) would be selected over a working docker or container runtime.

Now both functions call _runtime_is_connectable() which runs 'rt info'
(or 'container system status' for Apple) to verify the daemon is actually
reachable before selecting a runtime. yolo check also shows connectivity
status — broken runtimes get a warning instead of a green checkmark.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Apple Container find_running_container used unsupported --filter flag

'container ls --filter' doesn't exist in Apple's CLI — the error was
silently swallowed, causing find_running_container to always return None.
This made yolo remove and recreate the container on every invocation
instead of exec'ing into the running one.

Fixed by using plain 'container ls' (which lists running containers by
default) and scanning the output, matching find_existing_container's
approach. Also tightened the unit test to assert --filter is not used.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

All integration tests now run with the 'container' runtime when Apple

verification — all 10 tests pass: basic command, blocked tools, file
ownership, workspace mount, stale container recovery, --new flag, and
startup banner.
